finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_10.md:167; chapters/chapter_10.md:173; chapters/chapter_10.md:177; chapters/chapter_10.md:189; chapters/chapter_10.md:193; chapters/chapter_10.md:225; chapters/chapter_10.md:231
finding_results.notes: Mallick's accusation broken from one packaged two-part sentence into two separate beats (line 167 and 171), forcing Paul to react between them. Paul's confession now includes three unfinished lines showing pressure fracture: 'twisted around his—' (173), 'all over a—' (177), 'like an adjuster instead of a—' (177). The three-question volley (phone, cabinet, safe) split into separate exchanges (179-187) so each denial lands individually. Paul's inference about multiple tamperers now trails off unfinished: 'So other people were in there. Doing their own—' (193) rather than arriving as a packaged thesis. Removed narrator commentary admiring Paul's speech ('His answer came fast, stripped of qualifiers'). Mallick's closing analytical speech (formerly one long paragraph) broken into three beats (225, 229, 233) with Paul's reactions interleaved, including a pressure-true response: 'I know where I was' (231). The multiple-tamperer inference remains fully legible to the reader through the accumulated denials and Paul's unfinished arrival at the conclusion.

summary: Single finding addressed: the deck scene's key exchange roughened to show pressure through three unfinished lines in Paul's speech, Mallick's accusations and analysis broken into shorter beats with interleaved reactions, and narrator admiration of dialogue removed. The multiple-tamperer inference and leverage shift are preserved. No continuity, structural, or voice-signature changes outside the targeted exchange.
